jp: わたしなら 何処まで行ける？
視えるとこだけ見ないで
そんなの言えないよ 絶対
眠ることだけしないまま
街の灯（ひ）が消えるのを見てた
彼女では、入れない様な場所
虚しさを消せないまま
わたしだけ 何処まで行ける？
出来ることなら見ないで
そんな強くないよ 実際
先のことだけ見ないまま
したいことも我慢して
必死に役割り演じてた
ウソも「ほんとうだよ」と
わたしまだ 何処へでも行ける？
向き合えなくて、ごめんね
そんなに癒えないよ 一生
未だ夜は明けないから
暗がりに隠して終わる
彼女では、容れない様な人
多様な人や価値観があって
きっと多様な仲間外れがある
与えられた居場所が苦しかったら
そんなの疑ったって構わない
利き手を直したくない
変わるのは怖い　拒まれるのはもっと怖い
叫びたい言葉は輝いている
必要以上に自分を否定しないこと
心に抱えた尽（まま）だったわたし
スゴく遅くなったけど
en: Stretching on into the distance,
the road, its heat drained away,
stretching on and on,
this contrived emotion of mine,
at the edge of a wide world,
I was breathing.
The one who'd been crying out
was the self I kept pushed down.
The voice that's truly mine
won't come out unless I force it.
What's being asked of me,
which of it is it I prefer —
the self I couldn't quite become
I cursed, every time.
Without being able to see what's at my feet,
how far can I — myself — go?
Don't only look at the parts you can see;
I can't say something like that — never.
Doing nothing but not sleeping,
I watched the city's lights go out,
a place where, as her, I just couldn't fit.
The one who got hurt
was the falsified version of me.
Even a brooding face —
it's like I'm putting that on too.
What if, now — what if, back then,
I'd pushed myself harder?
The self I can't see through with
I'm still scolding even now.
Without being able to erase the emptiness,
how far can I — only me — go?
If you can help it, don't look at me;
I'm not that strong — really.
Doing nothing but not looking ahead,
I waited for the day to end,
a thing I can't tell anyone, can I.
Treated like I gave them the creeps,
holding back even what I wanted to do,
I was desperately playing my part
for the sake of — who, exactly?
I lied even with my lies, calling them "the truth,"
I was telling lies, and so
I end up kicking my own expectations away.
Even if I can't see what's at my feet,
can I — still — go anywhere I want?
I couldn't face it, I'm sorry,
it just won't heal that easily — for a lifetime.
Because the night still hasn't broken,
I'll hide it in the dark and let it end,
a person who, as her, just couldn't be held.
Because as him, I can't stay here —
There are all kinds of people and values,
and surely there are all kinds of being-left-out.
If the place you're given to belong is too hard to bear,
it's all right to doubt it.
I don't want my dominant hand corrected.
Changing is scary; being rejected is scarier.
The words I want to scream are shining.
Don't deny yourself more than you have to.
I'd been the me holding it all in my heart, just as it was —
it's gotten incredibly late, but —
I — right now —
can fit in.
